_comment: Generated by macro-analyst on 2026-04-30 (pre-open, Bucharest 07:36 EEST). MAJOR REGIME SHIFT: EUR/RON Apr 29 BNR fix at 5.1004 — broke 5.10 ceiling for first time after 30-day tight 5.08-5.0951 band → FX-3 ARMED. Motion of no-confidence FILED Apr 29 (PSD+AUR, 281 MP support vs 233 threshold) — vote scheduled May 5 (Tuesday, 3 trading sessions). Government fall now ~base case. BET bounced Apr 29 +0.75% to 28,603.69 from prior -2.67% on filing news — relief uncertainty; pre-vote re-derisking expected. Brent Apr 29 spiked +6.1% to 118.03 on Mid-East premium — pulled back to 113.60 intraday Apr 30 (-3.75%) → SNP whipsaw. TTF jumped Apr 28→29: 43.59→46.85 (+7.5% d/d, ~+4.4% wk) — still under €50/MWh trigger. VIX 18.81 (+5.5% d/d) still benign. DAX Apr 29 close 24,018.26 (-0.27% d/d, -0.73% wk) above 200DMA. RO 10Y Apr 29 7.33% (under 7.40% but +20bp 10d). NBR May 7 meeting in 7 days — 1 day BEFORE no-confidence vote could resolve; first cut still consensus but politically unstable backdrop weighs. CDS unavailable today. Active themes: Rating tail HIGHLY ELEVATED on coalition collapse risk (PNRR EUR 10bn at risk by Aug deadline, 9 reforms pending); MSCI June review intact; NBR pivot complicated by political risk; Post-election relief retired-grade. Regime caveat in force: 70% YoY rally to ATH biases mean-reversion. Source feeds: BNR XML (FX), Yahoo (^GDAXI, ^VIX, BZ=F, TTF=F, WIG20.WA), tradingeconomics WebSearch (RO 10Y, BET), Romania Insider/spotmedia (political).

_qualitative_context.note: Day 10 of coalition crisis. PSD+AUR motion of no-confidence FILED Apr 29 with 281 MP signatures (vs 233 threshold) — vote scheduled secret ballot Tuesday May 5. Government fall is now base case absent deal. PM Bolojan refusing resignation. PNRR EUR 10bn at risk by Aug 2026 deadline; 9 reform laws pending. EUR/RON Apr 29 BNR ref rate 5.1004 — first close above 5.10 in this cycle, breaking the 30-day tight 5.0801-5.0951 band → FX-3 ARMED at the threshold. RON breaking 5.10 historically precedes CDS blowouts (May 2025 5.00 break → 10Y to 8.0%). RO 10Y at 7.33% on Apr 29 (+20bp/10d), still under 7.40% RAT-1 trigger but momentum negative. BET Apr 29 closed 28,603.69 (+0.75%, partial relief from prior -2.67%). Brent +6.1% Apr 29 to 118.03 (Mid-East spike) then pulled back -3.75% to 113.60 in Apr 30 European hours — net SNP volatility, COM-1 daily-Δ trigger fired Apr 29 but reversing today. TTF +7.5% d/d to 46.85 — supply tightness, no named disruption flagged → COM-2 not armed (still <€50). VIX 18.81 still benign. NBR meeting May 7 sits ONE day after the no-confidence vote — first cut consensus but politics destabilizes the call. Active themes: (1) Rating tail HIGHLY ELEVATED — coalition fall + PNRR risk + EDP escalation cluster is exactly the configuration the reference doc flags as highest-risk for downgrade-to-junk. RATAG-2 not fired yet (no actual downgrade) but tripwire is hot. (2) MSCI June review intact. (3) NBR pivot complicated. (4) Post-election relief retired-grade. Regime caveat: 70% YoY rally to ATH biases mean-reversion; defensive lean.
_qualitative_context.regime_caveat: 70% YoY rally to ATH still active per reference doc — bias mean-reversion, skeptical of breakouts, weight relief-rally triggers. Coalition crisis layered on top; defensive cash bias appropriate.
